AAAS.name: EurekAlert!
AAAS.description: Science news releases from universities, journals, and research organizations.

AAAS.name: Science Magazine
AAAS.description: Science magazine news and research from AAAS.

Africanews.name: Africanews
Africanews.description: Pan-African news in English and French, part of the Euronews network

Al Jazeera.name: Al Jazeera
Al Jazeera.notes: Al Jazeera is funded by the Qatari government through the Qatar Media Corporation. Editorial independence is debated — RSF notes it 'reflects the Qatari government's viewpoint on regional issues' while acknowledging its professional journalism standards. Per CONTRIBUTING.md policy, labeled state-affiliated with this rationale documented.

ANSA.name: ANSA English
ANSA.description: Latest news from Italy and international politics, business, science, technology, lifestyle, and sports from ANSA

Asahi Shimbun.name: Asahi Shimbun — Japanese
Asahi Shimbun.description: Asahi Shimbun — major Japanese daily newspaper (Japanese edition).

Azadi Radio Pashto.name: Azadi Radio Pashto
Azadi Radio Pashto.notes:
Radio Free Europe / Radio Liberty's Afghan Pashto-language service. RFE/RL is funded by the U.S. Agency for Global Media but operates with editorial independence under the International Broadcasting Act. Provides news to Afghanistan from abroad since Taliban restrictions on independent media.

Sources:
  [1] https://pressroom.rferl.org/about-rferl
  [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radio_Free_Europe/Radio_Liberty

BBC.name: BBC News — Politics
BBC.description: UK and Westminster political news from the BBC.

BBC.name: BBC Science & Environment
BBC.description: BBC News science and environment coverage

BBC.name: BBC Sport
BBC.description: BBC Sport — live scores, match reports, analysis, and sports news

BBC News - Middle East.name: BBC News - Middle East
BBC News - Middle East.notes:
BBC regional bureau covering 15 Middle Eastern countries. BBC is the world's leading public service broadcaster, funded by the UK license fee with editorial independence enshrined in its Royal Charter. RSF ranks UK 24/180.

Sources:
  [1] https://rsf.org/en/country/united-kingdom
  [2] https://www.bbc.com/aboutthebbc/governance/charter

Bellingcat.name: Bellingcat
Bellingcat.notes:
Independent investigative collective based in the Netherlands. Specializes in open-source intelligence and fact-checking. Funded by grants and public donations. Multiple award-winning investigations including MH17, Skripal poisoning, and Syrian chemical weapons.

Sources:
  [1] https://www.bellingcat.com/about/
  [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bellingcat

Berkeley AI Research.name: Berkeley AI Research Blog
Berkeley AI Research.description: Research posts from the Berkeley AI Research (BAIR) lab.

Caixin.name: Caixin Global
Caixin.description: Caixin Global — Chinese financial and business news in English and Chinese.

Carbon Brief.name: Carbon Brief
Carbon Brief.description: A data-driven climate journalism blog that explains global warming through clear analysis of climate science, energy, and policy

CBC.name: CBC News — Top Stories
CBC.description: CBC News top stories — Canada's public broadcaster.

China Daily.name: China Daily
China Daily.notes:
State-owned English-language newspaper of the Chinese government. Published by the Publicity Department of the Chinese Communist Party. RSF ranks China among the most restrictive media environments globally.

Sources:
  [1] https://rsf.org/en/country/china
  [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/China_Daily

Chosun Ilbo.name: Chosun Ilbo
Chosun Ilbo.description: Chosun Ilbo — major Korean daily newspaper.

Climate Home News.name: Climate Home News

CNBC.name: CNBC Top News
CNBC.description: CNBC top business and market news.

Daily NK.name: Daily NK
Daily NK.notes:
Seoul-based online newspaper specializing in North Korea reporting through a network of citizen reporters inside the DPRK. NED-funded but editorially independent per its Journalism Ethics Charter. Foreign Policy Research Institute scholar describes it as an 'indispensable outlet' for North Korea coverage.

Sources:
  [1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daily_NK
  [2] https://www.dailynk.com/english/how-we-report/
  [3] https://grokipedia.com/page/Daily_NK

DB Corp.name: Dainik Bhaskar
DB Corp.description: Dainik Bhaskar — one of India's largest Hindi-language daily newspapers.

Engadget.name: Engadget
Engadget.description: Engadget — technology news, reviews, and buyer's guides.

European Space Agency.description: European Space Agency top news on space science and exploration.

Financial Times.name: Financial Times — World News
Financial Times.description: Financial Times world news RSS feed.

Folha.name: Folha de S.Paulo
Folha.description: Folha de S.Paulo — leading Brazilian daily newspaper.

Gizmodo.name: Gizmodo

Grist.description: Nonprofit environmental news outlet covering climate, sustainability, and environmental justice.

Hong Kong Free Press.name: Hong Kong Free Press
Hong Kong Free Press.notes:
Hong Kong-based non-profit, JTI-certified news outlet. No shareholders or share capital — run directly by journalistic staff. Journalism Trust Initiative certified in 2025 after 130-point external audit. SOPA award winner. Publishes annual financial transparency reports.

Sources:
  [1] https://hongkongfp.com/about/
  [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_Free_Press

Inside Climate News.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning nonprofit news organization covering climate change, energy and the environment.

International Consortium of Investigativ.description: ICIJ — global network of investigative journalists exposing crime, corruption, and abuse of power.

Investopedia.name: Investopedia
Investopedia.description: Investopedia financial education and market news.

IRNA News.name: IRNA News
IRNA News.notes:
Official state news agency of the Islamic Republic of Iran. Operates under the Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance, which appoints its leadership. State Media Monitor reports 80%+ of budget sourced from government.

Sources:
  [1] https://statemediamonitor.com/2025/07/islamic-republic-news-agency-irna/

  [3] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Republic_News_Agency

KFF Health News.description: Nonprofit newsroom covering U.S. health policy and care.

Live Science.description: Live Science — discoveries, technology, health, and the environment.

MarketWatch.name: MarketWatch — Top Stories
MarketWatch.description: MarketWatch top market and business stories.

Meduza.name: Meduza
Meduza.description: Meduza — independent Russian-language news outlet based in Latvia.

Mehr News.name: Mehr News
Mehr News.notes:
Mehr News Agency is under the Islamic Ideology Dissemination Organization (IIDO), an Iranian state body. State Media Monitor classifies both Tehran Times and Mehr News under IIDO oversight.

Sources:
  [1] https://statemediamonitor.com/category/globalliststatemedia/mena/middle-east/iran/

NK News.name: NK News
NK News.notes:
U.S.-based independent publication providing expert analysis and news on North Korea. Specialized in monitoring DPRK political, economic, and military developments. Cited by major outlets including Washington Post, BBC, and academic research.

Sources:
  [1] https://www.nknews.org/

Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting.description: OCCRP — worldwide investigative reporting on organized crime and corruption.

PBS.description: PBS NewsHour headlines — America's public television news program.

Roll Call.description: Capitol Hill and U.S. congressional news.

RT News.name: RT News
RT News.notes:
Russian state-funded international broadcaster. US State Dept (2024): 'de facto arm of Russia's intelligence apparatus.' Sanctioned by US, UK, EU, Canada. Banned globally by Meta and YouTube. Parent company TV-Novosti designated under E.O. 14024.

Sources:
  [1] https://apnews.com/article/russia-putin-trump-sanctions-harris-rt-f7e3ff262fdf24695048cffe2529b937
  [2] https://2021-2025.state.gov/alerting-the-world-to-rts-global-covert-activities/
  [3] https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/meta-bans-rt-russian-disinformation-rcna171402

SANA.name: SANA
SANA.notes:
Syrian Arab News Agency (SANA) is the official state news agency of the Syrian Arab Republic. State-run and controlled by the Syrian Ministry of Information.

Sources:
  [1] https://rsf.org/en/country/syria
  [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syrian_Arab_News_Agency

ScienceDaily.name: ScienceDaily — Health & Medicine
ScienceDaily.description: Health and medicine research news summaries.

ScienceDaily.name: ScienceDaily Top News
ScienceDaily.description: Breaking science news and articles on global warming, extrasolar planets, stem cells, nanotechnology, evolution — from the world's leading universities and research organizations

Simons Foundation.name: Quanta Magazine

Space.com.description: Space.com news covering space exploration, astronomy, and skywatching.

Spaceflight Now.name: Spaceflight Now
Spaceflight Now.description: Launch schedules, space mission coverage, and rocket news.

SpaceNews.name: SpaceNews
SpaceNews.description: SpaceNews covers space industry, policy, technology, and exploration.

STAT.name: STAT News
STAT.description: Reporting on health, medicine, and the life sciences.

TASS.name: TASS
TASS.notes:
Russian state news agency (TASS). Founded in 1904, government-owned and directly reflects Kremlin positions. Primary source of official Russian government statements and state narratives.

Sources:
  [1] https://rsf.org/en/country/russia
  [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TASS

TechCrunch.name: TechCrunch — AI
TechCrunch.description: Artificial-intelligence news and startup coverage from TechCrunch.

Tehran Times.name: Tehran Times
Tehran Times.notes:
English-language outlet under the Islamic Ideology Dissemination Organization (IIDO), a state body that supervises multiple Iranian state-linked media. State Media Monitor classifies it as state-controlled.

Sources:
  [1] https://statemediamonitor.com/category/globalliststatemedia/mena/middle-east/iran/
  [2] https://rsf.org/en/country/iran

The Bureau of Investigative Journalism.name: TBIJ
The Bureau of Investigative Journalism.description: The Bureau of Investigative Journalism — public interest investigative journalism.

The Decoder.name: The Decoder
The Decoder.description: English-language AI news and analysis published from Germany.

The Economist.name: The Economist
The Economist.description: The Economist business and finance section.

The Guardian.name: The Guardian - Iran
The Guardian.notes:
Part of Guardian Media Group, owned by the Scott Trust — a structure designed to protect editorial independence in perpetuity. No shareholders, no proprietor. RSF ranks UK press environment as 'satisfactory' (24/180).

Sources:
  [1] https://rsf.org/en/country/united-kingdom
  [2] https://www.theguardian.com/the-scott-trust

The Hill.name: The Hill
The Hill.description: U.S. political news covering Congress, the White House, and campaigns.

The Initium.name: The Initium
The Initium.description: The Initium — in-depth Chinese-language journalism from Hong Kong.

The Intercept.name: The Intercept
The Intercept.notes:
Independent non-profit investigative journalism outlet founded by Glenn Greenwald, Laura Poitras, and Jeremy Scahill. Pulitzer Prize-winning reporting (NSA surveillance/Snowden). Funded by First Look Media, a non-profit organization.

Sources:
  [1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Intercept
  [2] https://theintercept.com/about/

The Moscow Times.name: The Moscow Times
The Moscow Times.notes:
Independent Russian-language media relocated to the Netherlands after Russia's 2022 censorship laws imposing 15-year sentences for 'discrediting' the military. Declared 'undesirable organization' by Russian prosecutor general (July 2024), criminalizing cooperation. Reuters: 'one of the only Russia-based publications in English to maintain editorial independence.'

Sources:
  [1] https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russia-declares-independent-news-site-moscow-times-undesirable-2024-07-10/
  [2] https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/02/28/russia-blocks-number-of-independent-and-ukrainian-media-outlets-a76649

The Register.name: The Register
The Register.description: The Register — biting the hand that feeds IT.

Universe Today.name: Universe Today
Universe Today.description: Space and astronomy news from Universe Today.

VentureBeat.name: VentureBeat — AI
VentureBeat.description: Enterprise AI news and analysis from VentureBeat.

Wall Street Journal.name: WSJ Markets
Wall Street Journal.description: Wall Street Journal markets and finance coverage.

World Health Organization.name: WHO News
World Health Organization.description: Official news releases from the World Health Organization.

Xinhua News Agency.name: Xinhua English
Xinhua News Agency.description: Xinhua News Agency — China's official state news agency. Labeled state-affiliated per registry policy.

Yale University.name: Yale Climate Connections
Yale University.description: Yale program providing daily climate news and analysis.

Yonhap.name: Yonhap News
Yonhap.description: Yonhap News — South Korea's largest news agency (Korean edition).

ZDNet.description: ZDNet — technology news, reviews, and analysis for IT professionals.
